originalTitle: Shock as dozens of bodies, mostly infants, discovered in Kenya mass grave
title: Grown-ups Care for Tiny Ones in Kenya
content:
Hello everyone! Your friendly news reporter from Africa here. Today, we have a very important story from our beautiful country, Kenya. It's a story about helping little ones and making sure everyone is safe and cared for. Sometimes, grown-ups have to work very hard to understand things that are a bit sad, but they always do it with kind hearts.

A Special Discovery

Recently, in a place in Kenya, some special workers found something unexpected. They found some tiny babies and young children in a certain spot in the ground. It was a discovery that made everyone want to understand more. There were more than thirty little ones found, and most of them were very small babies and young children. This means grown-ups need to ask many questions and look for many answers. Did you know? Kenya is a big country in Africa! It has amazing animals like lions and elephants, and very kind people.

Who Were These Tiny Ones?

Many of the small children found were very, very little, like newborns. It makes us think about how precious every baby is. Grown-ups believe that these little ones might have come from places like hospitals. Hospitals are where doctors and nurses work very hard to help people when they are sick. Sometimes, sadly, tiny babies don't get to live for very long. When this happens, hospitals have special ways of caring for them before they are laid to rest. Did you know? Doctors and nurses are like superheroes! They work so hard to keep us healthy and help us when we are not feeling well.

Why Are Grown-ups Looking for Answers?

It is super important that we know why these tiny children were found in this special place. Every little person, even tiny babies who don't get to stay with us for long, deserves to be cared for properly and remembered with love. Grown-ups want to make sure that everything is done in the best way possible. They want to understand what happened so that it doesn't happen again. It's like solving a big puzzle to make sure everyone is safe and treated kindly. Did you know? Asking questions helps us learn and understand the world better! It's how we find out important things.

Police Are Like Detectives

When something like this happens, the police step in. The police are like super detectives! They wear special uniforms and work very hard to find clues. They talk to many people and look at everything very carefully. Their job is to find out the truth. They want to know why these tiny children were in that spot. They are checking hospitals and other places to make sure everything is okay. Did you know? The police help keep our communities safe. They are brave and work hard to protect everyone, big and small!

Helping Families Find Peace

When a baby or child passes away, it is a very sad time for their family. Families want to know where their little one is and that they are taken care of respectfully. The grown-ups who are investigating this are also working to help these families. They want to make sure that if any of these tiny children belong to a family that has been looking for them, those families can get the answers they need. This helps families to find peace and say goodbye properly. Did you know? Giving comfort and care to people who are sad is a very kind thing to do. It shows we care about each other.

What Happens Next?

The investigation will take some time, just like solving any big puzzle. The police and other grown-ups will keep working very hard. They will make sure that all hospitals and places that care for people, even after they pass away, follow all the rules. They want to make sure that every little child is treated with kindness and respect. It's about making our world a safer and more caring place for everyone, especially our littlest ones. Did you know? Even though this story is a bit sad, it shows how many grown-ups care deeply about children and work hard to make things right.

A Message for You

It's important to remember that grown-ups are always working to keep children safe. If you ever feel worried or have questions, you can always talk to a grown-up you trust, like your parents, teachers, or a kind aunt or uncle. They are there to listen and help you understand. This story reminds us that every life is precious, and we must always be kind and look out for each other. Your friendly reporter from Africa, sending you big smiles and warm wishes!
summary: Grown-ups in Kenya are working hard to understand why tiny babies and children were found in a special place, making sure everyone is safe and cared for.
whyMatters: It is important because every child is special, and grown-ups want to make sure all little ones are looked after properly and kindly.

originalTitle: Ghana and EU sign landmark defence deal to combat militant Islamist threat
title: Ghana and EU sign landmark defence deal to combat militant Islamist threat
content: Ghana is the first African country to make such a deal with the EU, the Ghanaian vice-president says.

originalTitle: Salah to leave Liverpool at end of season
title: Our African Star Salah Says Goodbye to Liverpool!
content:
Hey there, amazing kids of Africa! Get ready for some big news from the world of football! We have a super special hero from our very own continent. His name is Mohamed Salah, and he's from Egypt. He plays football like a superstar! He makes us all so proud, doesn't he?

Who is Mohamed Salah?

Mohamed Salah is a famous football player. He is super fast! He runs like the wind on the field. He scores many, many goals. When he plays, everyone cheers! He is a star from our home, Africa. He was born in a country called Egypt. Many people call him 'The Egyptian King' because he is so good at football. He is not just a great player, but also a kind person. He loves his country and helps many people there. He is a true hero, on and off the pitch. He shows us that with hard work, you can become the best in the world!

His Team: Liverpool

For many years, Salah played for a super famous team called Liverpool. This team is from a country far away called England. Liverpool wears bright red shirts when they play. They have a big stadium where many fans come to watch them. Salah helped Liverpool win many important games. He helped them win big trophies, like the Champions League and the Premier League. These are very special awards for football teams! The fans of Liverpool loved Salah so much. They would sing his name very loudly. They made him feel like a king every time he played.

The Big News: Salah is Saying Goodbye

Now, we have some news that might make some people a little sad, but it's also a part of life. Mohamed Salah has said he will leave Liverpool. This means he will not play for the red team anymore after this season ends. A 'season' is like a school year for football players, from summer to summer. He said this in a very kind way. He told all the fans that they gave him 'the best time of my life'. Can you imagine? Playing football and having the best time ever! He also said they 'stood by me in the toughest times'. This means when things were hard, or when he didn't score a goal, the fans still cheered for him. That's true love and support! It's like when your friends always cheer for you, even if you make a mistake in a game. Salah knows how important that support is, and he wanted to thank everyone who believed in him.

What Salah Taught Us

Salah has shown us that hard work pays off. He trained very hard to become such a great player. He never gave up, even when it was difficult. He always tried his best to score goals and help his team win. This is a very important lesson for all of us, kids. Whatever you do, try your best and never give up on your dreams! He also showed us how important it is to be thankful. He thanked the fans for all their love. It's good to say thank you to people who help you and cheer for you. Being polite and grateful makes you a great person, just like Salah.

Did You Know?

* Did you know that football is the most popular sport in Africa? Millions of kids, just like you, love to kick a ball around and dream of being a star! * Did you know Salah often visits his hometown in Egypt? He even helps to build schools and hospitals there! He uses his fame to help others, which is amazing and shows his big heart. * Did you know Salah has won an award called the Golden Boot many times? This award is for the player who scores the most goals in a football league. Wow! He is really good at finding the back of the net.

What Happens Next for Our Hero?

So, what will happen next for Mohamed Salah? We don't know exactly where he will play next. Maybe he will go to a new team in a different country. Or maybe he will take a little break. But one thing is for sure: he will keep playing football! He loves the game too much to stop. He will keep making magic with his feet and scoring amazing goals. We will still cheer for him, no matter which team he plays for. He will always be our African hero. We can't wait to see his next adventure! He will always carry the pride of Africa with him wherever he goes.

Keep Dreaming, Kids!

Mohamed Salah shows us that anyone can achieve great things. If you have a dream, work hard, be kind, and never give up. One day, you could be a star just like him! Keep playing, keep learning, and keep shining, amazing kids of Africa!
summary: Our football hero from Egypt, Mohamed Salah, is leaving his team Liverpool, but he thanked the fans for all their love and support.
whyMatters: This news is important because Salah is a big star who makes Africa proud, and he shows us that working hard can make your dreams come true, no matter where you play.

originalTitle: ‘Do not call your defeat an agreement’ IRGC warns US, Israel
title: News from Far Away: When Countries Talk!
content:
Hello, Amazing Kids!

Hi everyone! It's your friendly news reporter for Africa here, ready to tell you about something happening far, far away. Sometimes, big countries, just like big brothers and sisters, have important things to talk about. Today, we're going to learn about a country called Iran and some other countries called the United States (we call it the USA for short) and Israel. It's a bit like when you and your friends have different ideas, and you need to figure out how to be kind and fair to each other. Don't worry, we'll make it super easy to understand!

Meet the Countries!

Let's meet our friends in this story! First, there's **Iran**. Imagine a beautiful country with very old stories, ancient buildings, and lovely deserts and mountains. Iran is a country in a part of the world called the Middle East. Many, many people live there, and they have a rich culture with amazing food and music. Next, there's the **United States of America**, or the **USA**. This is a very big country far away, across a huge ocean. It has tall cities with big buildings, huge farms where lots of food grows, and lots of different people from all over the world live there. The leader of the USA is called a President. Then there's **Israel**. This is another country, also in the Middle East, not too far from Iran. It's also a place with ancient history and beautiful lands, especially around the sea. It has its own special stories and many different people too. Did you know? Africa is home to 54 wonderful countries, each with its own special stories and people! Just like these countries, every country in the world is unique and has its own leaders and ways of doing things.

Who are the IRGC?

Now, let's talk about the **IRGC**. This stands for the "Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps." That's a very long name, isn't it? For kids, you can think of them like special helpers or protectors for the country of Iran. They are part of Iran's army and they help keep the country safe from danger. They are very important in Iran and help to make sure the country's rules are followed and its people are protected. Sometimes, when a country wants to say something very important to another country, these special protectors might be the ones to speak for their country. It's like your school prefects who help make sure things run smoothly and pass on important messages from your teachers. Did you know? Most countries have armies or special guards to help keep their people safe and sound. Their job is to protect their homes and their friends, and to help in emergencies. It’s a very important job!

Talking or Not Talking? That's the Question!

Sometimes, leaders from different countries talk to each other. This is called having "talks" or "discussions" or sometimes even "negotiations." Imagine you and your friend are playing with a ball, and you both want to decide what game to play. You talk about it, right? You share your ideas and listen to theirs. That's like "talks" between countries. The leader of the USA, who used to be called President Trump, said that maybe Iran and the USA were having some talks. He suggested they were discussing important things to find solutions. But then, a helper from the IRGC in Iran said, "No, that's not true!" They said they were *not* having talks with the USA. It's like if your friend said, "We talked about playing football," but you knew you hadn't talked about it yet. So you would say, "No, we didn't!" That's what happened here. Iran's helper said they did not agree with President Trump's idea that talks were happening. Did you know? Sometimes, leaders might have different ideas about what is happening, and it's important for everyone to say what they truly believe.

A Special Kind of Warning

The IRGC helper also used some strong words. They said to the USA and Israel: "Do not call your defeat an agreement." Wow, those are big words for little ears! Let's make it super simple. Imagine two teams playing a game, like football or netball. If one team wins and the other loses, the losing team might feel a bit sad or like they "lost." That's like "defeat." An "agreement," on the other hand, is when both teams decide something together, and everyone is happy with the decision. Both sides feel like they won something or found a good solution. What the IRGC helper was trying to say is that Iran believes it hasn't "lost" anything, and if there are any big changes or decisions to be made, they must be something Iran agrees to, not something that feels like a loss forced on them. They want to be treated fairly, like an equal player in the game, where everyone gets a say. It's like saying, "We haven't lost, and if we make a plan, it must be a plan we *both* decide is good, not just what you want or what you think we should do." Did you know? Most of the time, countries try to solve their problems by talking nicely and finding a common ground, even when they disagree. This helps everyone stay safe and be friends, like good neighbours!

Why is this Important for Peace?

When countries talk, it's usually a very good thing! It means they are trying to understand each other and find ways to live together peacefully. Talking can stop little problems from becoming big problems. But when one country says they are having talks, and another says "No, we are not!" it can feel a bit confusing and make it harder to trust each other. The IRGC's words were a "warning" to the USA and Israel. A warning is like when your parents tell you, "Be careful near the fire, it's hot!" to make sure you don't get hurt. Here, the IRGC was telling the USA and Israel to be careful about how they talk about Iran and what they say about any talks. They wanted to make sure everyone knew Iran's side of the story very clearly. This is important because everyone wants to avoid big arguments or fights between countries. People want to live in peace, and clear communication is a big part of that. Did you know? People all over the world, including children like you in Africa, want peace and kindness between all countries. Talking fairly and honestly is a big step towards that wonderful goal!

Let's Talk!

In life, whether it's between friends, families, or big countries, talking is super important. When we talk, we can share our feelings, explain what we want, and listen carefully to what others want too. This helps us solve problems without getting upset or fighting. It helps us understand each other's views, even if they are different from ours. Even if these big countries are saying different things right now about "talks," the most important thing is that they keep trying to communicate and find ways to be peaceful. Everyone wants a world where countries can be friends and help each other, sharing ideas and resources. Think about how you talk to your friends. If someone says something you don't agree with, you would gently say, "Actually, I think it's like this..." That's what Iran's helper did here, in a very formal way for countries. It shows they want their voice to be heard clearly.

Our Wish for Peace

So, what does this all mean for you? It means that even far away, leaders of countries are always trying to figure out how to get along. Sometimes it's easy, and sometimes it's hard, just like making new friends. But with kindness, listening, and honest talking, people can always find a way forward to build bridges, not walls. Let's all wish for peace for Iran, the USA, Israel, and all the countries in the world, including our beautiful continent of Africa. May they always find ways to talk things out, understand each other, and be good neighbors and friends! We hope that one day, everyone will agree on talks that bring smiles, friendship, and a happy future to all.
summary: A special helper from the country Iran said they were not having talks with the USA, even though the USA's old leader had suggested they were, and Iran wants to make sure any decisions are fair and agreed upon by everyone.
whyMatters: It's important because when countries talk honestly and clearly, it helps them understand each other, be friends, and avoid big arguments, which keeps everyone safe and peaceful around the world.

originalTitle: Iran’s new security boss Mohammad Zolghadr: Why his appointment matters
title: A New Helper to Keep Everyone Safe in Faraway Iran!
content:
Hello Friends! Let's Learn About News!

Hello, amazing friends from all over Africa! It's your friendly news reporter here. Today, we're going on a special journey to learn about something important happening in a country far, far away. It's a place called Iran. Isn't it exciting to learn about different places in our big, wonderful world? Sometimes, grown-ups have big jobs that help keep countries running smoothly. These jobs are super important, just like your teachers help you learn in school, or your parents help keep your home safe and happy. Today, we're talking about a very special job in Iran. We'll learn about a new person who has a big task: to help keep all the people in Iran safe. Let's find out more about this new helper!

What is a "Boss" and Why Do We Need One?

First, let's talk about the word "boss." What does it mean? A boss is like a leader. They are in charge of something important. Maybe your head teacher is the boss of your school, or your mom or dad is the boss of what happens at home! A boss helps make sure everything works well. They listen, they plan, and they help solve problems. When there's a big job to do, it's good to have a good boss to lead the way. It helps everyone feel calm and know what to do. Countries also need bosses for different things. Some bosses help with schools, some help with roads, and some help with keeping everyone safe. It's a very big responsibility!

Meet Mr. Zolghadr!

Now, let's talk about the special person in Iran. His name is Mohammad Zolghadr. Can you say that with me? Mohammad Zol-ghadr! Mr. Zolghadr has just gotten a very, very important job. He is going to be the new "security boss" for Iran. That means he will be in charge of making sure everyone in Iran feels safe and sound. It's a bit like being the chief protector of the whole country! Before this big job, Mr. Zolghadr was a leader in the army for a long time. So, he knows a lot about keeping people safe and solving problems. He has lots of experience helping his country. He is ready for this big new challenge!

What is "Security"? It Means Being Safe!

So, what does "security" mean? It's a big word, but it's easy to understand! Security means being safe. It means feeling protected from harm or danger. Think about it – when you are safe, you feel happy, right? In your home, your parents make sure you have food and a safe place to sleep. That's a kind of security. At school, your teachers make sure you learn in a safe classroom. That's also security! For a whole country, security means making sure all the people can live without too much worry. Mr. Zolghadr's job is to help create this safety for everyone in Iran. He will work with others to make sure the country is strong and its people are protected. It's a big job to make sure a whole country stays safe and peaceful. Did you know? Every country in the world has people who work very hard to keep their citizens safe. It's a very important job for leaders everywhere!

Iran: A Special Place

Let's talk a little more about Iran. Iran is a big country in a part of the world called Asia. It has many, many people living there, just like our countries here in Africa have many people. Iran has beautiful mountains, old cities, and kind people. Imagine a country with lots of history and culture, where families live and children play, just like you do. All these people want to feel safe and happy in their homes and their towns. That's why having a good security boss is so important. Did you know? Iran is home to some of the world's oldest cities and amazing historical places! People have lived there for thousands of years.

Big Jobs and Big Challenges

Now, even when a boss tries their best, sometimes there are challenges. Challenges are like big puzzles or problems that need to be solved. For Mr. Zolghadr, his job comes with some big challenges. Sometimes, countries have disagreements with other countries. It's a bit like when friends don't agree on something, but on a much bigger scale. There are some problems that Iran has with other countries, like the United States and Israel. These are countries far away, and they don't always agree on everything. Mr. Zolghadr will need to help Iran manage these disagreements wisely. Also, sometimes people inside a country might not be happy. They might have problems they want their leaders to fix. This is called "internal unrest." It means some people are feeling sad or worried about things in their own country. Mr. Zolghadr will also need to help listen to these worries and help find solutions for the people of Iran. Did you know? Talking things through peacefully is always the best way to solve problems, whether it's between friends or between countries!

Why This Job is Super Important

So, why is this new job for Mr. Zolghadr so important? Because when a country feels safe, people can be happy. Children can go to school, parents can go to work, and everyone can live their lives without too much fear. Good leadership in security can help keep everyone calm and help solve problems before they get too big. Mr. Zolghadr will be working hard to make sure Iran can be a safe and stable place for all its citizens. His work can help bring peace and quiet to the country. It's a very big responsibility to look after the safety of so many people, and it shows how much leaders care about their country. We hope he does a super job!

Everyone Wants Peace!

No matter where you live in the world, whether it's in Africa, Asia, or anywhere else, everyone wants the same things: to be safe, to be happy, and to live in peace. When people work together and leaders make good choices, it helps everyone achieve this. Mr. Zolghadr's appointment is a step in that direction for Iran. It's about a country choosing a new leader to help guide them towards a safer future. We hope he will do a wonderful job protecting the people of Iran.

Let's Wish Them Well!

So, my dear friends, that's our news for today from faraway Iran! We learned about Mr. Mohammad Zolghadr, the new security boss, and why his job is so important. He's a leader who will help keep everyone safe and happy. Let's send our good wishes to Mr. Zolghadr and all the people of Iran, hoping for peace and safety for everyone. Until next time, stay curious and keep learning about our amazing world!

originalTitle: US-Israel war on Iran: What’s happening on day 26 of attacks?
title: A Friendly Chat About Peace in Our World!
content:
Hello, My Amazing Friends!

Hi everyone! It's your friendly African news reporter here, ready to share some important news with you. We live on a big, beautiful planet, and sometimes, even in far-off places, grown-ups have big problems. Today, we're going to talk about some grown-up problems happening in countries very, very far from our amazing continent of Africa. We'll learn about them together, and most importantly, we'll talk about how everyone hopes for peace and kindness. It’s like when you and your friend have a disagreement on the playground. It can feel big, right? But then you try to find a way to make it better. Countries sometimes have even bigger disagreements, and we want to understand a little bit about them and wish for everyone to be safe and happy.

Our Big, Wide World

Did you know that our Earth is a giant ball with so many different countries and people? It's like a giant puzzle with many pieces. We live in Africa, which is a wonderful, huge piece of that puzzle! Far away from us, across big oceans and lands, there are countries like Iran and Israel. And there's also a big ocean area called the Gulf. These places are very far away, but they are still part of our one world. Imagine looking at a globe – Africa is here, and these countries are on another side. Each country has its own people, its own homes, and its own special stories. Just like you have your family and your friends, people in these countries have theirs too. They all want to live safely and happily, just like us. *Did you know?* There are almost 200 countries in the world! That's a lot of different people and places, all living together on our one planet!

When Friends Disagree

Sometimes, even the best friends have disagreements, right? Maybe you both want to play with the same toy, or you can't agree on which game to play. When this happens, it can make you feel a bit sad or even a little cross. Well, sometimes, countries have disagreements too. These are big disagreements between grown-ups who lead these countries. They might disagree about rules, or land, or things they think are important. When countries have these big disagreements, it can sometimes lead to problems that make people feel unsafe and worried. Everyone wishes that grown-ups could always talk things out with kind words, just like we try to do with our friends.

Flying Objects in the Sky

Now, let's talk about something a bit serious, but we’ll explain it simply. In some of these places, like Iran and Israel, and in the Gulf area, there have been things flying in the sky. These are not happy kites or beautiful birds, or even airplanes carrying people on holidays. These are things called "missiles" and "drones." Imagine a very big, fast, unmanned toy airplane – that could be like a drone. And a missile is like a very powerful, fast rocket. Grown-ups sometimes use these flying objects when their disagreements get very big and serious. They are used to send a strong message, but they can also accidentally land in places where people live and cause damage. This makes many people feel scared and very, very sad because homes can get broken, and people can get hurt. It's a very serious problem, and nobody wants these kinds of flying objects to be used. *Did you know?* Kites were invented thousands of years ago in China! They were used for fun, for measuring things, and even for sending messages, but always in a gentle way.

What's Happening Far Away?

So, what's been happening in these far-off places? For about 26 days now – that’s almost four weeks! – some of these serious flying objects, the missiles and drones, have been seen in the sky in Iran, Israel, and near the Gulf. Another big country called the United States, or the US, has also been involved in these discussions and problems. When problems like these happen for many days, it means that the grown-ups are still trying very hard to find a solution. It's like when you have a tricky puzzle that takes a long time to solve. Everyone hopes they can solve this puzzle soon so that peace can return.

Why Are People Worried?

When grown-ups in different countries keep having big disagreements and using those serious flying objects, it’s called "military escalation." This just means that the problems are getting bigger and more serious, and people are taking bigger, more serious actions. Imagine if your playground disagreement kept getting bigger and bigger, and instead of talking, friends started shouting louder or throwing sand. That would make everyone feel very worried and unsafe, wouldn't it? Well, "military escalation" is like that, but much, much bigger and more serious for entire countries. It makes everyone around the world wish even harder for people to stop and talk instead. *Did you know?* Talking and listening are two of the most powerful tools we have to solve problems! When we really listen to each other, we can often find a way to be friends again.

Hope for Talking and Solutions!

But here's some really good news! Even when things are very serious, there's always hope. The grown-ups in the United States have said something important. They have "signalled openness to a deal." What does "openness to a deal" mean? It means they are ready and willing to talk! They want to sit down and find a way to stop the problems and make things better. It’s like when you and your friend finally decide to talk calmly about why you’re upset and find a way to share the toy or play a new game together. Talking is the best way to solve problems, and it means that people are trying to find a peaceful end to the disagreements. This is a very important step towards making everyone safe and happy again.

What We Wish For

From our amazing continent of Africa, we always wish for peace everywhere. We know that when people are kind to each other, listen to each other, and talk about their problems, the world becomes a much happier place. We hope that the grown-ups in Iran, Israel, the US, and all the countries around the Gulf will keep talking and keep working towards a good "deal" or solution. We hope they can put an end to the flying objects that cause sadness and damage, and instead, focus on building bridges of friendship and understanding. Every child, every family, and every country deserves to live in peace, without fear or worry. *Did you know?* Africa is the second-largest continent in the world! It has 54 beautiful countries, all hoping for peace and friendship with the rest of the world.

Let's Be Kind to Everyone!

So, my wonderful friends, remember that even when there are big problems in the world, there are always people trying to make things better by talking. Let's send our kindest wishes to everyone in Iran, Israel, the US, and the Gulf, hoping they find peace soon. And let's remember to be kind and understanding to everyone we meet, because that's how we build a happy and peaceful world, starting right here in our own homes and communities. Thank you for listening, and keep spreading kindness!
summary: For 26 days, grown-ups in countries like Iran and Israel have had big disagreements that sometimes involved serious flying objects, but now they are willing to talk to find a peaceful solution.
whyMatters: It's important because when countries have problems, people can feel scared and unsafe, and we all wish for everyone to live peacefully and happily together in our big world.

originalTitle: 'He liked the fear in our eyes,' Epstein survivors tell BBC
title: Brave Grown-Ups Share Their Stories: Your Voice is a Superpower!
content:
Hello, Super Friends from All Over Africa!

Welcome to our special news report! Today, we have a very important and heartwarming story to share with you. It's all about being brave and knowing that your feelings matter a lot. We're going to talk about some grown-up ladies who are sharing their stories to help everyone understand how important it is to talk to a grown-up you trust if you ever feel sad, worried, or scared. Imagine you have a big secret, or something makes you feel a bit yucky inside. Sometimes, we might think we need to keep these feelings or secrets all to ourselves. But these brave ladies want to tell you that this is not true! Sharing your feelings is like opening a window and letting sunshine in. When we talk about things that bother us, it's like magic! Our hearts feel lighter, and our smiles come back. These grown-up ladies are showing us all that when we speak up, we become stronger, and we can help others too.

Why Talking is Super Important

Sometimes, things happen that make us feel a bit wobbly or unsure. It could be a little thing, or something that makes your heart feel heavy. When these feelings come, it's like a cloud covering the sun. But guess what? You have the power to make that cloud go away! The first step is always to talk. Think of it like this: if you have a puzzle piece that doesn't fit, you ask a grown-up for help, right? Your feelings are a bit like that puzzle. If something doesn't feel right, a grown-up can help you sort it out. It’s super important to remember that if someone makes you feel sad, scared, or uncomfortable, it is *never* your fault. Grown-ups are there to protect you and make sure you feel safe and happy. You are special, and you deserve to feel good. Did you know? Our feelings are like different colors! We can feel happy (like bright yellow), sad (like soft blue), or even a bit scared (like dark grey). All feelings are okay to have, and it’s always good to talk about them. Sharing helps you understand them better.

Brave Ladies Share Their Feelings

There were five grown-up ladies who decided to share their stories with a special news program, just like our news show! They talked about times when someone was unkind to them, which made them feel very sad and scared for a long time. It was a time in their lives when they didn't feel very good inside. They told everyone how important it was for them to finally talk about these feelings. When they kept it all inside, it was like carrying a very heavy backpack all day long. Their hearts felt heavy, and it was hard to feel bouncy and happy. They didn't know what to do at first, and it felt like a big secret was hiding inside them. But when they started to talk to other trusted grown-ups and friends, a wonderful thing happened. The backpack started to feel lighter! They realized they weren't alone. Other people understood how they felt, and they started to feel stronger and happier again. Talking helped them find their sunshine again. These ladies are so brave because it takes a lot of courage to share a story that made them feel sad. But they did it because they want to help YOU and other children know that speaking up is always the best thing to do. Their bravery is like a bright lamp for everyone.

Finding Your Super Helper

So, who can be your super helper? It's any grown-up you trust with your whole heart! Think about the people who love you and always want to keep you safe. These people are your superheroes without capes! It could be your mommy or daddy, your grandma or grandpa, your favorite auntie or uncle, or even your teacher at school. It could be a wise elder in your community or a kind leader at your mosque or church. These are your 'trusted grown-ups.' They are the ones who always have time to listen. Your super helper will always listen to you very carefully. They will believe you and help you figure out what to do. They won't get angry with you. They will just want to help you feel safe and happy again. You can even draw a picture or use puppets to show how you feel if words are too hard at first. Sometimes drawing helps us show big feelings. Did you know? In many places in Africa, we have a strong community spirit! This means that many grown-ups in your village or neighborhood care about you and want to help keep you safe. Don't be shy to reach out and tell them if you need help!

Helping Others Feel Brave Too

These five brave ladies are like superheroes! By sharing their own stories, they are sending a powerful message to everyone: 'It's okay to talk! Your voice is important!' They are helping to make the world a safer place for everyone. They want to make sure that other children and grown-ups who might feel sad or scared know that they are not alone. They want to show everyone that talking about unkind things can help make those things stop. Their stories are like bright lanterns, showing the way for others to find their own brave voices. When one person is brave enough to speak, it gives courage to many more people. It's like a ripple in a pond – one small splash can create big waves of change and help many people feel safer and happier. Your voice, even if it feels small, can make a big difference.

What Happens When We Talk?

When you share your feelings with a trusted grown-up, amazing things start to happen. First, they will listen with their whole heart. They will make you feel safe and understood. They will hold your hand (or give you a hug if you want one!) and let you know it's okay. Then, they can help you understand that it's okay to feel whatever you're feeling. They will help you find solutions to problems that are making you sad or scared. If someone is being unkind, your trusted grown-up can help make sure that the unkindness stops and that you are safe. They can talk to other grown-ups to make sure everything is sorted out for you. Talking helps your heart feel lighter, your mind feel clearer, and helps you remember how special and important you are. It's like having a big hug for your feelings! It helps you feel like yourself again, the happy, bouncy you! Did you know? Sharing your worries with a trusted friend or grown-up can actually make your brain feel better! It helps us think more clearly and find solutions to problems, just like cleaning a messy room helps us find things.

Remember Your Super Powers!

So, remember this, my dear friends: you have super powers inside you! * **Your Voice is Your Super Power!** Don't be afraid to use it to share your feelings, especially if you feel sad, scared, or uncomfortable. Your voice helps you and helps others. * **Your Trusted Grown-Ups are Your Super Team!** They are always ready to help and keep you safe. They are like your shield and your guide. * **You Deserve to Feel Safe and Happy!** Every single day, you deserve to feel loved and cared for. This is a very important truth to remember. These brave ladies remind us all that talking is a powerful way to heal and to help others. Let's all be kind to each other and always, always listen to our hearts and our trusted grown-ups. Until next time, stay safe and keep shining brightly!
summary: Five brave grown-up ladies are sharing their stories to teach everyone that it's important to talk to a trusted adult if you ever feel sad or scared, because speaking up helps you and others feel safe and happy.
whyMatters: This story matters because it teaches all children to be brave and share their feelings with a trusted grown-up if someone makes them feel bad, helping them stay safe and happy.

originalTitle: Danish PM fails to secure majority in party's weakest election showing since 1903
title: Denmark's Boss Needs Help
content:
Hello from Africa!

Hello, super friends! It’s your kid-friendly news reporter here, all the way from Africa! Today, we have a really interesting story from a country far, far away. It’s a place called Denmark. Can you say Denmark? Den-mark! It’s in a part of the world called Europe, where it can get very cold and snowy!

Imagine a country that looks like a beautiful picture book, with old castles and lots of green fields. That’s Denmark! And just like our countries here in Africa, Denmark has special leaders who help make sure everything works well for the people.

Who is the Boss of Denmark?

Every country needs a boss, right? Someone who helps everyone work together. In Denmark, the main boss is called the Prime Minister. Her name is Mette Frederiksen. Can you say Mette Frederiksen? She has a very important job, like being the head teacher for the whole country!

She leads a special team of people. This team is called a political party. Think of it like a sports team, but instead of playing football, they work to make the country a good place for everyone. Her team is called the "Social Democrats."

Did you know? Denmark is famous for being a very happy country! People there often ride bicycles everywhere. And guess what else comes from Denmark? LEGO! Yes, those colourful building blocks you love to play with!

What is an Election?

Sometimes, grown-ups in a country get to choose who they want to be their leaders. This special day is called an election. It’s like when you vote for your favourite game to play, but much bigger! On election day, people go to a special place and put a paper with their choice into a box. It's a way for everyone to have a say!

Not long ago, Denmark had an election. People went to vote for their favourite teams, or "parties," and who they wanted to be the boss of the country. It’s a very important day because it helps shape the future of Denmark.

What Happened in the Election?

So, what was the big news? Mette Frederiksen’s team, the Social Democrats, got the most votes! Yay for them, right? It means more people chose her team than any other single team. That’s a good thing!

However, there’s a little puzzle. Even though they got the most votes, her team did not get something called a "majority." Hmm, what does "majority" mean?

What is a Majority?

Let’s imagine you have a big bag of yummy sweets, and there are 10 sweets in the bag. If you get 6 sweets, you have more than half, right? That means you have a "majority." You have enough to be the clear winner and share them as you like.

But what if you only got 4 sweets? That’s still the most, because maybe your friends got 3, 2, and 1. You have more than anyone else, but you don't have more than half of all the sweets. That means you don't have a majority.

In the election, Mette Frederiksen’s team got the most "sweets" (votes), but not enough to be more than half of all the votes. So, they didn't get a majority.

Why is a Majority Important?

When a team has a majority, it’s much easier for them to make big decisions for the country. They don't have to ask too many other teams for help with every single thing. They can just say, "This is what we want to do!" and it usually happens.

But when a team doesn't have a majority, like Mette Frederiksen's team, it’s a bit trickier. They have to talk to other teams. They need to find some other teams to work with them to make decisions. It's like needing extra friends to help you carry a very big box!

What Happens Next?

Because Mette Frederiksen’s team didn’t get a majority, they now have a big job to do. They need to talk, talk, talk! They will talk to other teams and say, "Will you join us? Will you help us lead Denmark?" This is called "forming a new government."

Forming a government means putting together all the people who will work hard to run the country. It's like choosing all the helpers for a big school play – you need actors, people for the lights, people for the music, and people to make the costumes!

These talks can be "tough talks," which means it might take a little while. They need to agree on what big plans they will all work on together for Denmark. It’s like when friends try to decide which game to play, and everyone has a different idea!

A Long, Long Time Ago

The news also said that this was the "weakest election showing" for her team since 1903. Wow! 1903 is a very, very long time ago. Imagine your great-grandparents, or even their parents, were tiny babies or not even born yet! That’s how old 1903 is!

It means that usually, Mette Frederiksen's team gets more votes and a clearer win than this. So, this election was a bit different and surprising for them. It’s like a famous football team not scoring as many goals as they usually do.

Did you know? Denmark has a Queen, even though a Prime Minister runs the government. The Queen is like a very special grandmother for the whole country!

Working Together is Key!

So, even though it’s a bit of a puzzle for Denmark right now, leaders are very clever. Mette Frederiksen and her team will work very hard. They will talk to other teams and try to find good friends to help them lead the country. It shows that even in faraway places, working together is super important!

It’s important because everyone wants what’s best for Denmark, just like we want the best for our countries here in Africa. Leaders all over the world learn to talk, share, and find ways to help everyone live a good life. That’s the news from Denmark today! See you next time, super friends!
summary: The leader of Denmark, Mette Frederiksen, and her team got the most votes in an election but not enough to win easily, so now they need to talk to other teams to help lead the country.
whyMatters: This is important because when leaders need to work with many teams, they have to talk a lot and agree on things, which shows that working together is key to making a country run well for everyone.

originalTitle: Meta told to pay $375m for misleading users over child safety
title: Meta told to pay $375m for misleading users over child safety
content: The owner of Instagram, Facebook and WhatsApp has been found liable by a court in New Mexico.

originalTitle: 'It wasn't meant to end like this' - Salah exit tough but inevitable
title: Our African Football Hero, Mohamed Salah, is Starting a New Adventure!
content:
Hello, amazing kids of Africa! It's your friendly reporter here, bringing you some news about one of our biggest stars. We are talking about the super-duper footballer, Mohamed Salah! He is a hero from Egypt, and he plays football like magic. Today, we have a story about him and his football team, Liverpool. It's a little bit sad, but also very exciting for what comes next!

Who is Mohamed Salah?

Mohamed Salah is a very famous football player. He comes from a country in Africa called Egypt. He loves to play football, and he is super good at it! Many people all over the world know his name. When he plays, he runs super fast and scores many goals. He makes us all proud to be from Africa! Did you know? People sometimes call Mohamed Salah the 'Egyptian King'! This is because he is so good at football and helps his country shine.

A Super Footballer from Africa!

Imagine kicking a ball so well that it always goes into the net! That's what Mohamed Salah does. He has fast feet and a very clever mind for football. When he plays, the crowd cheers very, very loudly! He makes football fun to watch for everyone. He has won many shiny awards for being such a great player. He is a true superstar! He shows all kids that if you work hard, you can be very good at something you love. He started playing football when he was a little boy, just like some of you might be doing now with your friends.

Playing for Liverpool

Mohamed Salah played for a big football team called Liverpool. This team is from a faraway country called England. Liverpool wears red shirts, and their fans love them very much. Their home stadium, where they play, is called Anfield. It's a very big place where thousands of people come to watch football. Salah helped Liverpool win many important games and shiny trophies. He was a very important part of their team. Did you know? A 'stadium' is like a super big playground with lots of seats for people to watch games!

Signing a Special Paper (Contract)

Less than one year ago, Mohamed Salah signed a special paper. This paper is called a 'contract'. A contract is like a big promise. When a footballer signs a contract, it means they promise to play for that team for a certain number of years. It’s like promising your friends you will play with them every day for a whole year. So, Salah promised to keep playing for Liverpool. Everyone was very happy about this big promise! When he signed this paper, he was very happy. He sat on a big chair, like a king, because he was staying with his team. The fans were cheering and celebrating! They thought he would be with them for a very long time.

A Big Surprise!

But now, there is some news! Even though he signed that special paper not so long ago, Mohamed Salah is going to leave Liverpool. This might sound a little bit confusing or even a little bit sad to some fans. Everyone thought he would stay longer. Why is he leaving now if he made a big promise? This is the big question everyone is asking. It's like when you love your school, but then you finish one class and need to move to another. It's a change, and sometimes change can feel a little strange at first. But change can also be good!

Why He is Leaving Now

Sometimes in football, things change. Even with a contract, players might decide they want to try new adventures. Or, sometimes the team decides it is time for new players. It's like when you grow up, and you want to try new things, even if you loved your old things. This is a big decision for Mohamed Salah and for the Liverpool team. It wasn't an easy choice for anyone, but sometimes changes just have to happen. It's part of the journey in life, and in football! Did you know? Football players often move to different teams to experience new challenges and make new friends in other cities or countries.

Saying Goodbye is Hard

Saying goodbye to a friend or a team you love can be tough. The fans of Liverpool are probably feeling a bit sad because they love Salah so much. He scored so many goals for them! But they will always remember the amazing times they had with him. And Salah will always remember the love from the fans at Anfield. It is okay to feel sad, but it is also okay to look forward to new beginnings. He has left a big mark on the team, like drawing a beautiful picture that everyone will remember for a long, long time.

What Happens Next?

So, what does this mean for Mohamed Salah? It means he will go to play for a new football team! He will still be the amazing goal-scorer we all know and love. He will make new friends on his new team and score even more fantastic goals. He will keep making Africa proud, no matter which team he plays for. We will all watch him and cheer him on, just like always! Did you know? Even if a player leaves a team, the fans usually still remember their good times and celebrate their success!

Mohamed Salah is Still a Star!

Mohamed Salah is a true inspiration. He is from Africa, just like us! He shows us that with hard work and a big dream, anything is possible. Even though he is leaving Liverpool, he will always be a football king. He will keep shining bright on the football field, making goals and making history. Let's keep cheering for our African hero, Mohamed Salah, wherever he plays! He is starting a new, exciting chapter in his amazing football story, and we can't wait to see what he does next! Thank you for listening, kids! Stay tuned for more amazing news from Africa!
summary: Our African football hero, Mohamed Salah, is leaving his team, Liverpool, for a new adventure, even though he signed a new contract recently.
whyMatters: This matters because Mohamed Salah is a very famous and important football player from Africa, and his move means he will play for a different team, bringing new excitement and challenges for him and his fans.

originalTitle: £123 for a child's England kit - have prices gone too far?
title: Oh No! Are Football Clothes Too Pricey for Our Pockets?
content:
Hello, my amazing friends! It's your kid-friendly news reporter here, bringing you some news from all over our wonderful continent, Africa, and beyond! Guess what? We all LOVE football, right? Kicking a ball with our friends, running around, scoring goals, and cheering for our favourite teams! It's super fun to play, and super fun to watch too!

What is a Football Kit?

When you watch your favourite football players on TV, or even when you play with your friends, you might see them wearing special clothes. These special clothes are called a 'kit'. A kit usually has a jersey (that's the shirt), shorts, and socks. They often have bright colours and cool designs. Lots of kids in Africa and all over the world dream of wearing a kit just like their football heroes. Imagine running around feeling just like your favourite player! It makes you feel strong and fast, doesn't it? Did you know? Football is super, super popular in Africa! Almost everyone loves to play or watch it. It brings families and friends together for lots of fun!

The Big Problem: Expensive Clothes!

Now, here's a little bit of sad news. Some of these special football clothes, especially the ones for big tournaments like the World Cup, are starting to cost a *lot* of money. A really big lot of money! So much money that it can be tricky for families to buy them. Imagine saving your pocket money for a whole year, and maybe even longer, just to buy one shirt! That's how expensive some of these kits are becoming. It makes grown-ups wonder if the prices are fair for everyone.

Why Do Kits Cost So Much?

Let's think about why these clothes might be so pricey. Well, the companies that make them, they use very special cloth. This cloth helps the players stay cool and dry when they run super fast. It's fancy fabric! Also, big famous companies (we call them brands) make these kits. These brands spend a lot of money to design the clothes and to get famous players to wear them. This makes the clothes even more special, but also more expensive. Did you know? The World Cup is a super big football competition that happens only once every four years! Teams from all over the world, including many great teams from Africa, play to see who is the best! Every time a big competition like the World Cup comes, the companies often make *brand new* kits. They change the colours a little, or add a new pattern. This makes people want the *newest* kit, but it also means they have to pay a new, sometimes higher, price.

What Does This Mean for Us Kids?

Sometimes, if a kit costs too much money, it can make kids feel a bit sad. They might really, really want to wear their favourite team's colours but their family can't afford it. That's a normal feeling. But guess what? It's super important to remember that not having the special, super expensive kit is totally, totally okay! You don't need the fanciest clothes to be a great football player or a super fan. Your love for the game and your team is what matters most! You can still play football, cheer for your team, and have fun with your friends.

What Can We Do?

So, what can we do if we really want to show our team spirit, but the kits are too expensive? Here are some ideas, friends! 1. **Talk to Grown-Ups:** You can talk to your mum, dad, or other grown-ups in your family. They might have ideas or suggestions. Sometimes, a plain t-shirt in your team's colours is just as good! 2. **Save Your Money:** If you get pocket money, you could save it up very slowly. But remember, saving for something so expensive might take a long, long time. It's okay if you decide to save for something smaller and quicker instead! 3. **Wear Team Colours:** You can find a regular t-shirt, shorts, or even a cap that is the same colour as your favourite team. For example, if your team wears green, you can wear any green shirt! It shows you support them just as much! 4. **Get Creative!** You could even draw your team's badge on a plain piece of paper and pin it to your regular shirt. That makes it your *very own* special kit! Did you know? Some families buy kits that are 'pre-loved' or 'second-hand'. This means someone else wore them a little bit, and then you can buy them for much less money! They are still perfectly good for playing in! 5. **Share with Friends:** Maybe a friend or an older brother or sister has a kit they don't wear anymore. You could share! Sharing is caring, and it's also smart for saving money.

Remember What's Important!

Dear friends, remember that football is about more than just fancy clothes. It's about playing together, learning to share, being kind to each other, and having lots and lots of fun. It's about the joy of running, jumping, and trying your best. The clothes don't make you a good player; your heart and your effort do! So keep playing, keep cheering, and keep smiling! Being a true fan means loving the game and your team, no matter what you are wearing. Let's keep loving football and playing fair! That's all for today from your kid-friendly reporter! Keep shining bright, and see you next time!
summary: Special football jerseys and shorts are getting very expensive, making it hard for kids to buy them, but loving the game is more important than fancy clothes!
whyMatters: It's important because every child should feel like they can be part of the football fun, and we need to remember that playing and having fun together is more important than expensive clothes.

originalTitle: Women's game needs more respect with officiating - Bompastor
title: Women's game needs more respect with officiating - Bompastor
content: Chelsea manager Sonia Bompastor says the women's game needs "more respect" after her side have a goal controversially disallowed in their Women's Champions League quarter-final first leg against Arsenal.
